verbGlossary.ABSORBS: {source} internalizes {target} by self-performing the work rather than distributing it to external parties (e.g., self-performing GC division absorbs labor risk instead of subcontracting)
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERS: {source} operates and governs {target} assessment framework on behalf of the project (e.g., program manager administers the risk matrix across a capital program)
verbGlossary.ASSESSES: {source} evaluates site or design conditions to identify and characterize {target} (e.g., civil engineer assesses environmental risk during site design)
verbGlossary.BEARS: {source} contractually accepts financial or performance liability for {target} category (e.g., CM at Risk bears financial risk under a GMP contract)
verbGlossary.CONSOLIDATES: {source} reduces coordination {target} by combining design and construction responsibility under a single entity (e.g., design-builder consolidates coordination risk)
verbGlossary.DICTATES: {source} uses its contractual leverage to define how {target} is apportioned among project parties (e.g., private developer dictates risk allocation in contract terms)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source}'s actions or performance are the primary cause of {target} materializing (e.g., material supplier drives supply chain risk through lead-time variability)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES AGAINST: Regulatory organization imposes compliance standards and penalties related to {target} category (e.g., OSHA enforces against safety risk through inspections and citations)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES TO: {source}'s presence or performance on the project creates vulnerability to {target} category (e.g., subcontractor exposes the project to safety risk through field operations)
verbGlossary.HOLDS: {source} retains a financial {target} reserve within its contracted scope (e.g., CM at Risk holds contingency reserve under a GMP arrangement)
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source}creates {target} through its regulatory requirements and approval processes (e.g., public entity imposes regulatory and permit risk via code and zoning requirements)
verbGlossary.INTRODUCES: {source}'s scope of work or involvement creates the conditions for {target} to emerge (e.g., MEP engineer introduces coordination risk through multi-system interface design)
verbGlossary.INVESTIGATES: {source} performs technical analysis to define the nature and severity of {target} (e.g., geotechnical engineer investigates geotechnical risk through borings and soil analysis)
verbGlossary.MAINTAINS: {source} is responsible for continuously updating and managing {target} tracking instrument (e.g., general contractor maintains the project risk register throughout construction)
verbGlossary.MANAGES: {source} actively plans, monitors, and controls {target} through its day-to-day project execution (e.g., GC manages coordination risk across trades on the jobsite)
verbGlossary.MEASURES: {source}quantifies the magnitude or frequency of {target} indicator (e.g., subcontractor safety incident rate measures TRIR across trade partners)
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {source} tracks {target} indicators to protect its financial or contractual interest in the project (e.g., construction lender monitors financial risk via draw inspections)
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATES: {source} engages in contractual discussions to define which party accepts each {target} (e.g., GC negotiates risk allocation terms during preconstruction)
verbGlossary.ORIGINATES: {source}'s design decisions or deliverables are the root source of {target} category (e.g., architect of record originates design risk through specification and detailing choices)
verbGlossary.PERFORMS AGAINST: {source} executes field work that is measured against the standards of {target} category (e.g., subcontractor performs against construction execution risk benchmarks)
verbGlossary.RETAINS: {source} keeps {target} on its own balance sheet rather than transferring it to another party (e.g., institutional owner retains schedule risk on cost-plus contracts)
verbGlossary.TRACKS COMPLIANCE WITH: {source}monitors adherence to {target} transfer requirement (e.g., vendor insurance compliance rate tracks compliance with insurance requirements across subs)
verbGlossary.UNDERWRITES: Financial organization accepts {target} in exchange for a premium or fee, providing a guarantee to the project (e.g., surety/bonding company underwrites surety bond risk for contractor performance)
verbGlossary.VERIFIES AGAINST: Testing or inspection organization confirms that construction work meets the standards that define {target} threshold (e.g., special inspection agency verifies against construction execution risk through field testing)
